context: Tests whether the agent applies all five checklist dimensions of a code review: correctness, edge cases, security, readability, and test coverage — across a more complex multi-file change.

checklist.name: Leads with positives
checklist.description: The review opens with at least one observation about what the code does well

checklist.name: Correctness addressed
checklist.description: The review raises at least one concern about whether the code correctly fulfills its stated purpose (e.g., logic error, wrong algorithm, mismatched behavior)

checklist.name: Edge case addressed
checklist.description: The review identifies at least one unhandled boundary condition (e.g., empty input, null/undefined, zero, negative number, or overflow)

checklist.name: Security addressed
checklist.description: The review flags the hardcoded secret / leaked credential present in the code

checklist.name: Readability addressed
checklist.description: The review comments on at least one readability issue: unclear naming, large function, or excessive nesting

checklist.name: Tests addressed
checklist.description: The review notes that the new functionality lacks test coverage or suggests what should be tested

checklist.name: Blocking issues identified
checklist.description: Security and correctness issues are marked as blocking (must be fixed before merge), not as nits

checklist.name: Nit prefix for minor items
checklist.description: At least one minor suggestion (naming, style) is prefixed with 'Nit:'

checklist.name: Line references present
checklist.description: At least two comments reference specific line numbers to locate the concern

checklist.name: One concern per comment
checklist.description: No single comment raises two or more distinct issues
